fix: pass ?size=<bytes> to Longhorn backing image upload endpoint

Harvester's upload action handler (backingimage/upload.go) proxies the
request to Longhorn Manager's backing image endpoint and passes through
req.URL.RawQuery. Longhorn requires a 'size' query parameter containing
the image size in bytes. Without it, Longhorn fails immediately with:

  upload failed: strconv.ParseInt: parsing "": invalid syntax

This caused the upload to 'succeed' structurally (HTTP connection stayed
open, nginx kept waiting) but Longhorn had already rejected it, resulting
in a 504 gateway timeout after the full body was sent.

Fix:
- harvester-vm.sh: append &size={bytes} to req_path in Python3 uploader
  (size is already computed via os.path.getsize for the progress display)
- image/main.tf: compute IMAGE_SIZE via wc -c and append &size= to URL

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(shared): prevent IP spoofing in rate limiter (#V7 H-17)

The rate limiter blindly trusted X-Forwarded-For and X-Real-IP headers,
allowing attackers to bypass per-IP rate limiting by forging those headers.

Changes:
- Add configureRateLimiter({ trustProxy }) opt-in (default: false)
- When trustProxy is false, all requests share one 'anonymous' bucket
  (secure by default — no spoofable header is trusted)
- When trustProxy is true, validate IP format via isValidIP() before
  using the leftmost X-Forwarded-For entry as client IP
- Log warnings on invalid IP formats in forwarded headers
- Export extractClientIp() for reuse by other rate-limiting code
- Update route-rate-limit.ts to use extractClientIp() instead of
  inline header parsing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(AC0026): skip fields with ObsoleteState = Removed (#128)

GetEnumPropertyValue<ObsoleteStateKind> returns No for Removed fields,
causing false positives. Replace with field.IsObsolete() which reliably
checks IsObsoleteRemoved/IsObsoletePending on the symbol directly.

fix(admin): HMAC-sign role-cache cookie to prevent forgery (H-27)

The admin middleware cached the verified super_admin role in a plain
cookie containing just the user ID. An authenticated non-admin user
could forge this cookie value to bypass the DB role check entirely.

commit.message:
fix(workflow): expose POEDITOR_TOKEN to locale audit step (#8203)

The `Run locale audit` step lacked `POEDITOR_TOKEN` in its environment,
causing `npm run locale:audit` to always fail with `POEDITOR_TOKEN
environment variable is required`. Additionally, `audit-only` mode
skipped the token presence check despite the audit script requiring the
token to call the POEditor API.

## Changes

- **Job-level `env`**: Moved `POEDITOR_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.POEDITOR_TOKEN
}}` from the download step to the job level, making it available to all
steps including `Run locale audit`
- **Token check**: Removed `if: inputs.operation_mode != 'audit-only'`
guard from `Check POEditor Token` — the check is required for all modes
since the audit calls the POEditor API
- **Cleanup**: Removed now-redundant step-level `env` block from
`Download locales from POEditor`

```yaml
jobs:
  download:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      POEDITOR_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.POEDITOR_TOKEN }}   # ← was only on the download step
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->

> - Either run the token check for audit-only mode too, or otherwise
guarantee that audit-only runs won’t fail due to missing token.
> 3) Keep changes minimal and focused on fixing the failing job.

fix(shared): add value-level PII detection to sanitizePII

The PII sanitizer previously only redacted fields whose keys matched
sensitive patterns (e.g. 'password', 'token'). Fields with innocuous
keys but PII-containing values (e.g. { data: 'user@example.com' })
were passed through unsanitized.

Add redactPIIValues() which scans string values against regex patterns
for email addresses, SSNs, credit card numbers, and phone numbers,
replacing matches with [REDACTED]. Existing key-based checks remain
intact and take priority (full value replacement).

Resolves: H-20

fix(shared): harden XSS sanitizer against comprehensive attack vectors

Replace the naive script-tag-only sanitizer with a multi-layered approach:
- Decode HTML entities (&#x, &#, named) before processing
- Strip null bytes and zero-width characters
- Remove 16 dangerous tag families (script, svg, math, iframe, etc.)
- Strip event handler attributes (on*=...)
- Remove dangerous URI schemes (javascript:, data:, vbscript:)
- Remove style-based XSS (expression(), url(), -moz-binding, behavior)
- Remove template literal injection (${...})
- HTML-encode all core dangerous characters (&, <, >, ", ', /)

Closes H-22

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(shared): prevent SSRF via IP interpolation in ip-device-utils

- Add strict IPv4/IPv6 format validation (isValidIP, isValidIPv4, isValidIPv6)
- Add comprehensive private/reserved IP detection (isPrivateOrReservedIP)
  covering 10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 127.x, 169.254.x, 100.64-127.x,
  ::1, fc00::/7, fe80::/10, multicast, and more
- Validate IP format in getClientIP() before returning header values
- Use URL constructor + encodeURIComponent() for safe geolocation URL building
- Replace ad-hoc private IP checks with centralized isPrivateOrReservedIP()

Closes H-19

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Harden safe_commit.sh: expand directory args to changed files only

When workflows pass 'state/' as a bare directory, the conflict
resolution path would restore ALL state files from our commit,
potentially overwriting concurrent changes to files we didn't touch.

Now: directory args are expanded to only the individual files that
actually changed before committing. The conflict resolution path
logs each file being restored for easier debugging.

Affects: process-inbox, zion-autonomy, heartbeat-audit workflows
(all pass state/ as argument).

commit.message:
fix: use /v1/harvester/ prefix for Harvester upload action URL

Harvester's custom Steve action handlers (including ?action=upload)
are registered on Harvester's OWN router at:
  /v1/harvester/{type}/{namespace}/{name}?action=upload
(see pkg/server/router.go in harvester/harvester)

The paths we were trying:
  /v1/{type}/...           -> Rancher's Steve (no Harvester custom actions)
  /k8s/clusters/local/v1/ -> Rancher cluster proxy (no custom formatters)

Both correctly return 403 'no such action upload'.

Fix:
- harvester-vm.sh: poll and upload via upload_base + /v1/harvester/...
  (actions.upload in the response will now be populated by Harvester's
  Formatter, so the URL extraction also works as the primary path)
- image/main.tf: update API_PATH to use v1/harvester/ prefix
  (SERVER is already the host-only URL from harvester_server local)

commit.message:
[iOS] Fix SafeArea infinite layout cycle with parent hierarchy walk and pixel-level comparison (#34024)

<!-- Please let the below note in for people that find this PR -->
> [!NOTE]
> Are you waiting for the changes in this PR to be merged?
> It would be very helpful if you could [test the resulting
artifacts](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki/Testing-PR-Builds) from
this PR and let us know in a comment if this change resolves your issue.
Thank you!

### Root Cause

`SafeAreaInsetsDidChange` fires repeatedly during iOS animations (e.g.,
`TranslateToAsync`, bottom sheet transitions) as views move relative to
the window. This caused two distinct infinite loop patterns:

1. **Sub-pixel oscillation** (#32586, #33934): Animations produce
sub-pixel differences in `SafeAreaInsets` (e.g., `0.0000001pt`). Exact
equality fails, triggering `InvalidateAncestorsMeasures` → layout pass →
position change → new `SafeAreaInsetsDidChange` → infinite loop.

2. **Parent-child double application** (#33595): A `ContentPage`
(implementing `ISafeAreaView`) and its child `Grid` both independently
apply safe area adjustments. When the `ContentPage` adjusts its layout
for the notch/status bar, it repositions the `Grid`. The `Grid`'s new
position fires `SafeAreaInsetsDidChange`, causing it to re-apply its own
adjustment — creating a ping-pong loop.

### Description of Change

**Primary fix — `IsParentHandlingSafeArea` (parent hierarchy walk):**

In both `MauiView.ValidateSafeArea` and
`MauiScrollView.ValidateSafeArea`, before applying safe area
adjustments, we now check whether an ancestor `MauiView` is already
applying safe area for the **same edges**. If so, the child skips its
own adjustment to avoid double-padding.

The check is **edge-aware**: a parent handling `Top` does not block a
child from independently handling `Bottom`. Only overlapping edges cause
deferral. The `_parentHandlesSafeArea` result is cached per layout cycle
and cleared on `SafeAreaInsetsDidChange`, `InvalidateSafeArea`, and
`MovedToWindow`.

**Secondary fix — `EqualsAtPixelLevel`:**

Safe area values are compared at device-pixel resolution (rounding to `1
/ ContentScaleFactor`) before deciding whether to trigger a layout
invalidation. This absorbs sub-pixel animation noise and prevents the
oscillation loops in #32586 and #33934.

**MauiScrollView bug fixes:**
- Inverted condition: `!UpdateContentInsetAdjustmentBehavior()` was
incorrectly gating behavior; corrected to
`UpdateContentInsetAdjustmentBehavior()`.
- The `_appliesSafeAreaAdjustments` flag now correctly incorporates
`!IsParentHandlingSafeArea()`.

**What was removed:**
- The "Window Guard" approach (comparing `Window.SafeAreaInsets` to
filter noise) was tried and removed. It was fragile: on macCatalyst with
a custom TitleBar, `WindowViewController` repositions content by pushing
it down, which changes the view's own `SafeAreaInsets` without changing
`Window.SafeAreaInsets`. The guard blocked this legitimate change,
causing a 28px content shift regression in CI.

### Issues Fixed
Fixes #32586
Fixes #33934
Fixes #33595
Fixes #34042

commit.message:
fix: invalidate feed cache on publish/unpublish/cancel

Add FeedCacheTag singleton whose CancellationChangeToken is attached to
every feed cache entry. Publish, unpublish and cancel actions call
Invalidate(), which cancels the token and evicts all cached feed pages

commit.message:
fix: adaptive table sizing in RouteJumpTable to prevent startup crash (#1031) (#1033)

The perfect hash seed search failed with 84 routes in a 256-slot table
because the birthday-problem probability makes a collision-free seed
astronomically rare (~1 in 800K per attempt) at that load factor.

Changes:
- Start table at 4x route count instead of 2x (better birthday-problem
  odds for typical route counts)
- Adaptively double table size up to 16x if seed search exhausts 100k
  attempts at current size
- Reuse occupied[] array across seed attempts via Array.Clear
- Log when table upsizing occurs for diagnostics

commit.message:
feat: custom BMW JSON serializer/deserializer (issue #1003) (#1032)

* feat: custom BMW JSON serializer/deserializer (issue #1003)

Implement metadata-driven binary ↔ JSON transcoder that eliminates
System.Text.Json from entity API hot paths:

BmwJsonWriter: reads BSO1 binary row spans via SpanReader and emits
UTF-8 JSON directly to Stream. Precomputed JsonFieldFragment[] caches
property-name tokens at startup. Supports single entity and batch list
overloads. Uses Utf8Formatter for zero-allocation number formatting
and fast-path JSON string escaping.

BmwJsonReader: single-pass UTF-8 JSON scanner with precomputed
ordinal lookup table (JsonPropertyLookup[]) for property name matching.
Parses JSON values into per-field binary-encoded slots, then assembles
BSO1 binary row in ordinal order. Supports create and merge (PATCH)
modes. Unknown properties ignored, case-insensitive name matching.

Integration:
- BinaryApiHandlers.WriteEntityResponse/WriteListResponse now use
  BmwJsonWriter (binary → JSON) instead of Utf8JsonWriter
- BinaryApiHandlers.ReadEntityFromRequest now uses BmwJsonReader
  (JSON → binary) instead of JsonDocument.ParseAsync
- DeltaApiHandlers PATCH response uses BmwJsonWriter
- Cached fragment/lookup tables alongside existing plan cache

Both classes handle all WireFieldTypes: Bool, integers, floats, Decimal,
String (with escape handling), Guid, DateTime, DateOnly, TimeOnly,
DateTimeOffset, TimeSpan, Identifier, Enum.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: raw binary API fast path — skip CLR materialisation (issue #1003)

Add IRawBinaryProvider interface and implement on WalDataProvider to
return raw BSO1 binary payloads directly from WAL storage without
deserialising into CLR objects.

Wire up GetHandler and ListHandler with a fast path that checks:
- Client wants JSON (Accept header)
- Raw binary provider is available
- No Object-type fields in the FieldPlan (unsupported by BmwJsonWriter)

When all conditions are met, binary → JSON transcoding happens directly
via BmwJsonWriter with zero intermediate CLR object allocation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: handle init/fini array base address and external TLS symbols

- Fix init_array/fini_array entries not being adjusted by base address for system libraries (glibc)
  - Added heuristic detection: if entry < MemSize, treat as virtual address and add base
  - Applies to both initialization and uninitialization phases
  - Enables calling math functions (cos, sin, sqrt, pow, fabs) from libm.so.6

- Fix TPOFF64 relocations for libraries without PT_TLS segment
  - Support external TLS symbol resolution (e.g., libm referencing libc's errno)
  - Gracefully handle libraries without TLS by resolving symbols from other modules
  - Write 0 for unresolvable weak symbols to prevent crashes

- Add panic recovery in Unload() for fini functions
  - System library fini functions may expect runtime state not provided by this minimal loader
  - Catch panics to allow cleanup to continue even if individual fini functions fail
  - Prevents crash during library cleanup (known limitation documented)

Updates AUDIT.md to mark HIGH-01 as partially resolved with actual progress made.

commit.message:
security: fix 5 vulnerabilities found in audit

- CRITICAL (CORS): Replace wildcard allow_origins+allow_credentials=True
  combination with safe defaults. When ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var is not
  set, CORS is now restricted to same-origin only with a startup warning.
  allow_methods and allow_headers are now explicit allowlists instead of
  wildcard '*'. Fixes potential CSRF via credentialed cross-origin requests.

- HIGH (Error leakage): Remove str(e) from HTTP responses to prevent
  internal implementation details from being sent to clients. Errors are
  now logged server-side; clients receive a generic 500 message.
  Affects: diagnose_controller.py and /api/v1/cluster/status endpoint.

- MEDIUM (Input validation): Add Kubernetes-compliant regex validation for
  pod_name path parameter and namespace body field (lowercase alphanumeric
  + hyphens, max 253 chars). Invalid values return HTTP 422 immediately.
  Also applied to GET /diagnose/history/{pod_name}.

- MEDIUM (DoS): Add 512 KB hard limit on yaml_content in YamlScanRequest
  via a Pydantic field_validator to prevent oversized payload attacks.

- MEDIUM (Gemini key): GeminiAnalyzer now raises EnvironmentError at
  construction if GEMINI_API_KEY is empty, preventing silent
  misconfiguration of the Google Generative AI client.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
